title: ABI, Shared Libraries, and Foreign-Function Memory · Memory-Safety Defense
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar abi, shared libraries, and foreign-function memory decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for versioned cross-language ownership ABI. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Characterize calling convention, symbol visibility, integer widths, alignment, layout, and compiler flags for each supported ABI.
Expose stable opaque handles with versioned constructors, operations, observers, and same-library destruction.
Pass buffers with lengths, ownership direction, allocator family, and release callback across FFI.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Abi Layout Call Contract
steps.instruction:
Order the Abi Layout Call Contract evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Abi Layout Call Contract.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Abi Layout Call Contract assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Abi Layout Call Contract, keep Abi Layout Call Contract connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz native-systems team handles memory case 9049. Produce bounded C evidence to validate a versioned foreign buffer and allocator family; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Characterize calling convention, symbol visibility, integer widths, alignment, layout, and compiler flags for each supported ABI.
